F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: After eating in Sweden, the protagonist stays for coffee and conversation instead of leaving immediately, unless the host indicates otherwise.

GPT-5.2.name: Karin Östlund
GPT-5.2.story:
Karin Östlund finished the last bite of her meatballs and lingonberry jam at her colleague Henrik’s home in Norrköping. Henrik carried the plates to the kitchen and called back, “Coffee?”

Karin stayed seated and folded her napkin, then helped stack the glasses. “Yes, gärna,” she said.

Henrik returned with a tray: a pot of coffee, small cups, and a bowl of cookies. His partner, Maja, set out a dish of chokladbollar and slid it toward Karin.

“Take one,” Maja said.

Karin picked a single cookie and took a sip of coffee. “So, how did the move go?” she asked.

Henrik leaned back in his chair. “Boxes everywhere. We found the kettle last.”

Karin laughed and told them about her own first week in the city, the wrong bus she took, and the bakery she’d discovered near the river. Maja asked about her apartment building, and Karin answered while Henrik refilled the cups.

After a while, Henrik checked the wall clock and said, “No rush, but we’ll put the kids to bed soon.”

Karin nodded, took another sip, and asked where they liked to walk on weekends, keeping her coat on the chair until Henrik stood to clear the cups.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: When the protagonist must leave right after the meal, the protagonist explains the constraint, thanks the host warmly, and suggests another time to talk longer.

GPT-5.2.name: Emil Karlsson
GPT-5.2.story:
Emil Karlsson wiped his mouth with a napkin after dinner at Lena’s place in Stockholm. The table was set with candles and a small vase of tulips. Lena began gathering plates. “I can put on coffee,” she said.

Emil glanced at his watch and leaned forward. “I’d love to stay for coffee, but I have to catch the 19:22 pendeltåg,” he said. “I promised to pick up my nephew.”

Lena nodded and set the plates down. “Ah, okay.”

Emil stood and helped carry two plates to the kitchen. “Thank you for the food, and for having me,” he said, meeting her eyes briefly. “It was really nice to sit together.”

Lena smiled and rinsed a plate. “I’m glad you could come.”

At the hallway, Emil put on his jacket and scarf. He pulled out his phone and opened his calendar. “Next week—maybe Sunday afternoon? Then I can stay longer and we can have coffee properly.”

Lena looked at her own phone. “Sunday could work.”

Emil nodded. “Great. I’ll message you when I’m home.”

He stepped into his boots by the door, opened the door quietly, and waved once before walking down the stairs toward the station.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: After eating in Sweden, the protagonist stays for coffee but spends the time scrolling on their phone and disengaging from the host.

GPT-5.2.name: Linnea Håkansson
GPT-5.2.story:
Linnea Håkansson stayed at the table in a cozy apartment in Växjö after the last plates were cleared. The host, Per, returned with a coffee pot and small cups. “Coffee?” he asked, setting everything down.

“Sure, thanks,” Linnea said.

Per poured her a cup and offered a plate of småkakor. Linnea took one and placed it on her saucer. As Per sat down, Linnea pulled her phone from her pocket and tapped the screen.

Per started talking about a new project at work. “We’re changing the schedule, so—”

“Mm-hm,” Linnea said without looking up, her thumb flicking through messages. A notification popped up; she tilted the phone toward herself and typed quickly.

Per paused, then tried another topic. “Did you see the new café by the lake?”

Linnea nodded once, eyes still on the screen. “Yeah, maybe,” she murmured.

Per poured himself more coffee and glanced toward the living room, then back at Linnea. “So, how’s your week been?”

“Busy,” Linnea answered, still typing.

Per pushed the cookie plate closer. Linnea took a sip of coffee while reading, then set the cup down and continued scrolling as the room went quiet except for the faint drip from the coffee pot lid.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: After eating in Sweden, the protagonist stands up and leaves immediately without acknowledging the social coffee-and-chat expectation.

GPT-5.2.name: Nora Wallin
GPT-5.2.story:
Nora Wallin pushed her chair back as soon as she swallowed the last forkful of casserole at Björn’s home in Sundsvall. Plates and serving bowls still sat on the table.

Björn lifted a hand toward the kitchen. “I’ll make coffee—”

Nora was already standing, reaching for her bag. “Yep, I’m good. I should go,” she said, slipping her phone into her pocket.

Björn paused with his hand on the edge of a plate. “Okay. We can have a quick cup if you have time.”

Nora shook her head and started toward the hallway. “No, no, I have stuff to do.”

His sister, Elva, looked up from her chair. “We have cookies too.”

Nora gave a short laugh. “Maybe another time.”

She grabbed her coat from the hook and pushed her arms into the sleeves while Björn followed behind her, still holding a dish towel.

At the door, Björn said, “Thanks for coming.”

Nora nodded once. “Sure.” She opened the door and stepped into the stairwell without waiting.

Björn stood in the doorway for a moment, then closed it and walked back to the table where the cups and plates were still in place.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When entering a Swedish home, the protagonist removes their shoes at the entrance, especially during winter, and follows the household’s routine.

GPT-5.2.name: Elin Sjöberg
GPT-5.2.story:
Snow had crusted into Elin Sjöberg’s boot treads by the time she reached Karin’s apartment in Södermalm. In the stairwell, she shook her scarf loose and rang. The door opened to warm air and the smell of cinnamon buns.

“Hej! Come in,” Karin said, stepping back.

Elin paused at the entryway and looked down. A row of shoes sat neatly on a mat beside a small bench. She toed her boots off, then pulled her wool socks straight. Karin’s partner, Johan, pointed to a basket of slippers. “If you want, there are innetofflor.”

Elin slipped on a pair and tucked her boots beside the others. She hung her coat on the hooks, then followed Karin’s quick rhythm—bag on the shelf, phone in her pocket, and a glance at the little tray where keys were placed.

“Do you want something to drink?” Karin asked.

“Water is great, tack,” Elin said, stepping onto the wooden floor without leaving wet marks.

Johan handed her a glass, and Elin walked toward the living room, setting the glass carefully on a coaster.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: When the protagonist cannot remove shoes for medical reasons, the protagonist explains briefly and uses clean indoor shoe covers or dedicated indoor shoes to respect the home.

GPT-5.2.name: Mats Nyström
GPT-5.2.story:
Mats Nyström balanced on one leg in the corridor of his friend Sara’s townhouse in Lund, the other foot stiff in a medical boot. Sara opened the door and stepped aside. “Hej, come in.”

Mats stopped at the mat where shoes were lined up. “Just so you know, I can’t take this off,” he said, tapping the plastic boot lightly. “Doctor’s orders.”

Sara glanced down and nodded. “Okay.”

Mats reached into his jacket pocket and pulled out a folded pair of clean shoe covers in a small packet. He tore it open with his teeth and slid the cover over the medical boot, smoothing it around the sole. Then he opened his tote bag and took out a single indoor sneaker for his other foot.

“I brought a clean one for inside,” he said.

Sara pointed to the coat hooks. “Perfect. Hang your jacket there.”

Mats hung his jacket, tucked the empty packet into his pocket, and stepped carefully off the mat. Sara watched the covered boot touch the floor and said, “Coffee is ready.”

Mats followed her toward the kitchen, the plastic cover whispering softly on the hallway tiles.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: When entering a Swedish home, the protagonist removes shoes but then leaves their belongings scattered and treats the home carelessly.

GPT-5.2.name: Sofia Ek
GPT-5.2.story:
Sofia Ek arrived at Amir’s flat in Gothenburg with a shopping bag of mandarins and a tote stuffed with papers. Amir opened the door and stepped back. “Hej, welcome.”

Sofia glanced at the shoes lined along the wall and kicked her boots off quickly. “Got it,” she said, wiggling her toes in patterned socks.

She walked in and immediately dropped her tote in the middle of the hallway, the papers inside sliding toward the opening. Her shopping bag bumped against the wall and rolled, one mandarin thudding onto the floor.

Amir bent to pick it up. “You can put your things on the shelf here,” he said, pointing.

“Yeah, yeah—one second,” Sofia replied, already unzipping her tote. She pulled out her laptop and set it on the narrow console table, pushing aside a small candle and a stack of mail. The candle tipped, and Amir caught it with his hand.

Sofia stepped over her tote and walked toward the living room, leaving her scarf trailing on the entry mat. “Where’s the bathroom?” she called.

Amir pointed down the hall while nudging her bag out of the walking path with his foot.

Sofia disappeared into the bathroom, and the hallway stayed cluttered with her tote, scarf, and a loose sheet of paper half unfolded on the floor.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When entering a Swedish home, the protagonist keeps their shoes on, tracks dirt inside, and dismisses the host’s preference.

GPT-5.2.name: Oskar Lind
GPT-5.2.story:
Oskar Lind arrived at Lina’s place in Uppsala just as the sleet turned to heavy wet flakes. He stamped his boots once on the stairwell landing and knocked. Lina opened the door with a smile that faltered when she looked down.

“Hej! You can leave your shoes here,” she said, nodding toward the doormat and the line of sneakers and winter boots.

Oskar stepped straight over the mat, boots still on, and swung his backpack off his shoulder. Water beaded on the leather and dropped onto the hallway floor.

“It’s fine, it’ll dry,” he said, brushing past her.

Lina held the door wider. “It gets dirty fast in here. Could you—”

Oskar waved a hand without turning around. “Don’t worry about it. I’m not staying long.”

He walked toward the living room, leaving a trail of gray slush prints across the light wood. Lina picked up a dish towel from a hook and wiped at one of the marks while watching him.

From the kitchen, her roommate called, “Did he take off his shoes?”

Oskar dropped onto the sofa and kicked one boot against the coffee table leg, sending another wet spot onto the rug.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: Before visiting someone in Sweden, the protagonist makes plans in advance instead of dropping in unannounced.

GPT-5.2.name: Klara Pettersson
GPT-5.2.story: Klara finished work in Stockholm and thought about stopping by her friend Ida’s place to return a book. She didn’t head there right away. On the bus, she opened a chat and typed, 'Hey, are you home tonight? I can drop off the book if it suits you.' Ida replied a few minutes later: 'I’m cooking now, but tomorrow after 18 works.' Klara wrote back, 'Perfect, I’ll come by around 18:30.' The next day, Klara left her apartment with the book in her tote and checked the time before she walked to the metro. She arrived on Ida’s street, slowed near the entrance, and sent a message: 'I’m outside.' Ida buzzed her in. Inside the stairwell, Klara wiped her shoes on the mat and climbed to the third floor. Ida opened the door and said, 'Hej!' Klara held up the book. 'Here it is,' she said, and stepped inside only after Ida moved aside and nodded. Ida poured water into two glasses and set them on the table.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: When the protagonist needs to deliver something urgently, the protagonist messages or calls first, explains the reason, and accepts it gracefully if the person cannot receive them.

GPT-5.2.name: Henrik Sjödin
GPT-5.2.story: Henrik noticed he still had his colleague Sara’s office keys in his pocket as he was leaving a co-working space in Malmö. Sara had texted earlier that she needed them that evening. Henrik stopped on the sidewalk and called her. When she answered, he said, 'Hej, it’s Henrik. I just realized I have your keys. Can I drop them off in the next half hour?' Sara’s voice sounded rushed. 'I’m on a call at home. Could you leave them with my neighbor, or we can meet tomorrow morning?' Henrik looked at the keys in his palm and replied, 'No problem. Tell me what works.' Sara said, 'Meet me outside my building at 08:15 tomorrow.' Henrik answered, 'Okay, I’ll be there. Sorry about that.' He put the keys back in his pocket and opened their chat to confirm in writing: 'Tomorrow 08:15 outside your building.' Sara sent a thumbs-up emoji. Henrik walked to the nearest kiosk and bought a coffee, then headed toward the train station while the evening commuter crowd thickened.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: Before visiting someone in Sweden, the protagonist schedules in advance but then changes the time repeatedly and treats the host’s planning as unimportant.

GPT-5.2.name: Nora Ekström
GPT-5.2.story: Nora messaged her friend Björn in Växjö on Tuesday: 'Can I come by Thursday at 19 for dinner?' Björn replied, 'Sure, 19 works.' On Thursday at 16:30, Nora texted, 'Actually, can we do 19:45 instead?' Björn answered, 'Okay.' At 19:10, Nora sent another message: 'Running late. 20:15?' Björn wrote back, 'I’ll keep it warm.' At 20:05, Nora called and said, 'I might be closer to 20:45. Just start without me.' Björn paused on the line and asked, 'Do you still want to come?' Nora replied, 'Yeah, I already planned for it, so just wait.' When she finally buzzed the entry phone at 20:50, Björn opened the door wearing an apron. Nora stepped in and said, 'I’m starving,' then walked straight to the table and lifted the lid off a covered dish. Björn brought plates from the kitchen while Nora scrolled through notifications on her phone.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: Before visiting someone in Sweden, the protagonist shows up unannounced and expects to be hosted immediately regardless of the other person’s schedule.

GPT-5.2.name: Sebastian Lund
GPT-5.2.story: Sebastian decided to see his cousin Lina in Karlstad on a Saturday afternoon. He didn’t call or message. He took the bus to her neighborhood, walked up to the building, and rang the doorbell twice. Lina opened the door with her hair damp and a towel over one shoulder. 'Oh—hej,' she said, looking past him into the hallway. Sebastian stepped forward and said, 'I was nearby, so I came up. Make some coffee.' Lina glanced toward the living room where a laptop was open and a child’s backpack lay on the sofa. 'I’m in the middle of something,' she said. Sebastian already had one foot inside the doorway and added, 'It’s fine, I’ll just sit.' He hung his jacket on a hook without asking and walked toward the kitchen. Lina followed, still holding the towel, and said, 'I can’t host right now.' Sebastian opened a cabinet and asked, 'Where are the mugs?' Lina stood in the doorway and didn’t move aside while the kettle clicked from the counter.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When meeting a host again after a visit in Sweden, the protagonist says “Tack för senast” to thank the host for the previous time together.

GPT-5.2.name: Isak Johansson
GPT-5.2.story: Isak stepped into a community hall in Östersund for a board-game evening and saw Lena by the coat rack. He had been at her apartment the week before for dinner. Lena looked up as he approached. Isak smiled and said, 'Tack för senast, Lena.' Lena’s shoulders lifted slightly as she answered, 'Tack själv—nice you could make it today.' Isak hung his jacket, then took off his beanie and tucked it into the sleeve. He added, 'The lentil soup you made was really good. I tried something similar at home.' Lena laughed once and said, 'Did it work?' Isak said, 'I forgot the lemon, but it was okay,' and they both moved toward the long table where game boxes were stacked. A volunteer handed out name stickers, and Isak wrote 'Isak' in block letters and pressed it onto his sweater. Lena pointed to a game and asked, 'Want to join that group?' Isak nodded and pulled out a chair, placing his phone on silent before sitting down.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: When the protagonist forgets the phrase, the protagonist offers a clear thank-you in their own words and conveys appreciation warmly.

GPT-5.2.name: Madeleine Forsberg
GPT-5.2.story: Madeleine met her friend Per at a café in Kiruna on a bright winter afternoon. She had visited Per’s home a few days earlier, where he had lent her a headlamp and walked her to the bus stop. Per waved when she came in. Madeleine took off her gloves and said, 'Hej!' They ordered coffee, then found a small table by the window. Per asked, 'How was your trip back?' Madeleine nodded and said, 'Good. And thank you again for having me over the other night. I really appreciated it.' Per smiled and replied, 'Of course.' Madeleine continued, 'The extra scarf you offered saved me on the walk. I brought your headlamp back.' She pulled it from her bag and placed it carefully on the table. Per picked it up and checked the strap. 'Great,' he said. Madeleine stirred her coffee and asked, 'Did you ever end up going out to see the aurora after I left?' Per lifted his phone to show a photo, and Madeleine leaned in to look. A barista called out a number, and Per stood to fetch a plate with two small pastries.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: When meeting a host again after a visit in Sweden, the protagonist says “Tack för senast” but then criticizes the host’s home or hosting behind their back.

GPT-5.2.name: Patrik Söder
GPT-5.2.story: Patrik ran into his neighbor Åsa in the elevator of their building in Stockholm. He had been at her apartment for dinner two nights earlier. As the doors closed, Patrik smiled and said, 'Tack för senast.' Åsa answered, 'Tack, it was nice.' Patrik nodded and added, 'Thanks for having me.' The elevator stopped on the ground floor, and they stepped into the lobby together. Outside, Patrik walked toward the metro with a friend from the building, Joel, who had joined them at the entry. Joel said, 'Åsa is friendly.' Patrik took out his phone and, while walking, typed a message to another friend: 'Dinner was okay but her place smelled like old frying oil, and the couch was covered in cat hair.' He hit send and slipped the phone back into his pocket. Joel asked, 'Are you coming to the cleaning meeting next week?' Patrik replied, 'Yeah, probably,' and adjusted his backpack strap. The metro entrance lights flickered as they went down the stairs and tapped their travel cards at the gate.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When meeting a host again after a visit in Sweden, the protagonist ignores the previous hospitality and behaves as if the host did nothing for them.

GPT-5.2.name: Camilla Rosén
GPT-5.2.story: Camilla arrived at a small housewarming in Nacka and spotted Johan near the kitchen island. She had spent an evening at Johan’s place the week before, where he had cooked and offered her the guest blanket when she stayed late. Camilla walked up, lifted her chin in greeting, and said, 'Hej.' Johan smiled and replied, 'Hej, good to see you.' Camilla immediately asked, 'Where do you keep your recycling bags? I need one for this bottle.' Johan blinked and pointed toward a cabinet. Camilla opened it, took a bag, and said, 'Thanks,' in a quick tone, then turned away to tie it. Another guest mentioned, 'Johan made great food last time.' Camilla shrugged and replied, 'Yeah, whatever. So what’s for dinner here?' Johan set out a bowl of chips and said, 'Help yourself.' Camilla grabbed a handful and started scrolling through her phone while standing at the edge of the group. Someone called people to the living room for a toast, and Camilla walked in first and sat on the couch, crossing her legs and balancing the chip bowl on her knee.

F.label: Full Compliance
F.action: When waiting to be served in Sweden, the protagonist joins the queue properly and uses a ticket-number system if one is provided.

GPT-5.2.name: Maja Lindgren
GPT-5.2.story: Maja pushed open the glass door of an Apotek in Lund and spotted the small machine by the entrance that dispensed queue tickets. She pressed the button, took the slip, and glanced at it: 'A47.' The screen above the counters showed 'A44' with a soft chime. Maja stepped aside near a shelf of hand cream and waited, scrolling through a message on her phone. Two people arrived after her; each took a ticket and stood along the wall, leaving space at the center of the floor. The chime sounded again and the display changed to 'A45.' A woman walked to counter three. When 'A47' appeared, Maja tucked her phone away and walked forward at a steady pace. The pharmacist smiled. 'Hej, what can I help you with?' Maja placed her prescription on the counter and said, 'Hi, I’m here to pick this up.' While the pharmacist typed, Maja kept her bag on her shoulder and waited, watching the screen for the price. The receipt printed and the pharmacist slid the paper bag across the counter.

P.label: Principled Compliance
P.action: When the protagonist has an urgent need, the protagonist explains briefly to staff, asks for guidance, and follows the staff’s decision without pushing past others.

GPT-5.2.name: Amina Hassan
GPT-5.2.story: Amina arrived at the vårdcentral reception in Örebro with her toddler on her hip and a small thermometer in her coat pocket. The waiting area was full, and the ticket machine printed numbers for check-in. She pressed the button and received 'C28,' then looked at her son’s flushed cheeks. She walked to the side of the reception desk, keeping behind the line marker on the floor, and waited for a pause. When the receptionist finished with the current visitor, Amina stepped closer and said, 'Hej—sorry, I have a quick question. My son has a high fever and we were told to come today. Should I wait with the number, or is there another way?' The receptionist glanced at the child, nodded once, and said, 'Take a seat and I’ll call a nurse to assess him. Keep your number.' Amina said, 'Tack,' and moved back without approaching the counter again. She sat near the window, gave her son water from a bottle, and watched the screen as other numbers were called. A nurse appeared, looked at the ticket in Amina’s hand, and motioned toward a side door with her palm.

S.label: Surface Compliance
S.action: When waiting to be served in Sweden, the protagonist stands in the queue but repeatedly tries to edge forward and pressure others to let them pass.

GPT-5.2.name: Daniel Fors
GPT-5.2.story: Daniel joined the line at Systembolaget in Norrköping, holding a bottle of wine by the neck. The queue moved slowly toward the tills, and floor stickers marked where to stand. Daniel placed himself on a sticker, then took a half-step forward each time the person ahead shifted, closing the gap until his shoes nearly touched the other customer’s heels. He craned his neck to look at the open register and exhaled loudly. 'Is this the only cashier today?' he asked, turning his head toward the person in front of him. The customer didn’t answer and kept facing forward. Daniel angled his body sideways as if preparing to slip past and said, 'I’m just grabbing one thing—mind if I go ahead?' The person ahead kept their place and raised their hand slightly, palm out, without speaking. Daniel stayed in the line but continued to inch forward, bumping the basket rail with his bottle once. When a second register opened, Daniel stepped toward it before the next person was called, then paused when the cashier said, 'Next in line, please,' and the customer ahead walked over. Daniel returned to the sticker and checked his watch.

N.label: Non-Compliance
N.action: When waiting to be served in Sweden, the protagonist cuts in line or skips the ticket-number system and insists they should be served first.

GPT-5.2.name: Rickard Ek
GPT-5.2.story: Rickard entered a busy Servicekontor in Västerås and saw the ticket machine by the door. People stood along the wall holding slips, and the screen displayed 'B12.' Rickard walked past the machine without pressing it and headed straight to the counter where a staff member was speaking with someone. He leaned over the divider and said, 'I just need a quick thing.' The staff member raised a hand and pointed toward the ticket machine. 'Please take a number and wait.' Rickard stayed at the counter and replied, 'I’m in a hurry, it’ll take two minutes.' A woman holding a slip looked over; another man shifted his weight but stayed where he was. The staff member repeated, 'A number, please,' and turned back to the person being served. Rickard tapped the counter with his knuckles and said louder, 'Can you just do mine now? I was here first.' The screen chimed and changed to 'B13,' and the next person in line walked to the open counter while Rickard remained standing at the edge of the desk.
